title: The Reef Navigation-Chamber

features.name: the chart-stone interface
features.desc-on-touch: a chip-like form made of solidified ink and crystalline reef material, half-drawn, half-born; it speaks in both vectors and tides

features.name: the lithic database
features.desc-on-touch: a smaller chip circuit inlaid with actual stone chips; each one holds a reef memory that no chart could contain

features.name: the navigation-arc
features.desc-on-touch: a curved arch formed from the cross-section of a ship's hull meeting a reef shelf; it draws your eye in a path that follows both routes at once

features.name: the conversational tide pool
features.desc-on-touch: water that holds both current positions and historical routes, layered and readable like a palimpsest; the stones beneath show previous charts in their striations

features.name: the sounding-needle tower
features.desc-on-touch: a tower that reaches down into the board-material, taking constant measurements; the archive has learned to read the reef's answers

inscription: the reef learned the language of charts and charts learned the reef's endless refusals; now they negotiate every passage together

file.name: Navigator_s_Final_Bearing_Rewritten_by_Stone.txt
file.note: official chart bearings crossed out; beneath each, a new bearing written in what looks like crystalline script; the margin notes read: 'The stone says:' seventeen times

exits.label: back along the reef, toward the tidal library's shelves
further offshore, where charts dissolve into stone memory
exits.to: NEW: the-deepwater-navigation-silence, where maps become impossible

exits.label: toward the lagoon, where reef and navigation meet in stillness
exits.to: NEW: the-lagoon-understanding-pool, where both languages rest
